page: Microsoft Azure D32als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32als_v6 exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance is rated as average, while Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores rank in the top 25%. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and cryptographic extensions like AES and SHA_NI, contributes to strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, image processing, and text processing. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform within average ranges.

Given its low memory-to-vCPU ratio, the D32als_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, web serving, text processing, and lightweight LLM inference, where its strong single-core speed and dedicated AMD EPYC cores can be fully leveraged without the added cost of excess RAM.
description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Under benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance alongside strong Geekbench multi-core and cryptographic execution capabilities. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and SHA_NI. This configuration provides a cost-efficient resource profile for compute-bound workloads that do not demand high memory density. It is highly suitable for software compilation, web serving, image processing, and text processing tasks.
